description: Complete reference of FISH intrinsic functions available in PFC 5.0 for ball, wall, contact, and measurement operations.

sections.heading: Ball FISH Functions
sections.content:
General:
  ball.find(id)        — get ball pointer by ID
  ball.list()          — iterate all balls: loop foreach bp ball.list
  ball.num()           — total ball count
  ball.maxid()         — highest ball ID in use
  ball.near(pos)       — find ball nearest to position
  ball.near(pos, rad)  — find ball nearest to position, within radius tolerance
  ball.inbox(p1,p2,b)  — get balls within bounding box
  ball.id(bp)          — get ball ID
  ball.typeid(bp)      — ball type identifier

Geometry:
  ball.pos(bp, dir)    — position component (1=x, 2=y, 3=z)
  ball.pos.x(bp)       — x-position
  ball.pos.y(bp)       — y-position
  ball.pos.z(bp)       — z-position
  ball.radius(bp)      — ball radius
  ball.disp(bp, dir)   — displacement component

Physical:
  ball.density(bp)     — density
  ball.damp(bp)        — local damping coefficient
  ball.mass(bp)        — mass (may be scaled with set timestep scale)
  ball.mass.real(bp)   — real (unscaled) mass
  ball.moi(bp)         — moment of inertia (may be scaled)
  ball.moi.real(bp)    — real moment of inertia

Velocity/Force:
  ball.vel(bp, dir)    — velocity component (1=x, 2=y, 3=z)
  ball.spin(bp, dir)   — angular velocity component
  ball.force.app(bp, dir)     — applied force component
  ball.force.contact(bp, dir) — contact force sum component
  ball.force.unbal(bp, dir)   — unbalanced force component
  ball.moment.app(bp, dir)    — applied moment
  ball.moment.contact(bp, dir) — contact moment sum
  ball.moment.unbal(bp, dir)  — unbalanced moment

Stress (PFC5 specific - available via ball.stress):
  ball.stress(bp, 1,1) — sigma_xx
  ball.stress(bp, 2,2) — sigma_yy
  ball.stress(bp, 3,3) — sigma_zz
  ball.stress(bp, 1,2) — sigma_xy
  ball.stress(bp, 1,3) — sigma_xz
  ball.stress(bp, 2,3) — sigma_yz

Group/Extra:
  ball.group(bp, slot)              — get group name in slot
  ball.isgroup(bp, name, slot)      — check group membership
  ball.group.remove(bp, name)       — remove from group
  ball.extra(bp, slot)              — extra variable value
  ball.fix(bp, dof)                 — get fixity (bitmask: 1=x, 2=y, 4=z)
  ball.fragment(bp)                 — fragment ID
  ball.energy(name)                 — energy tracking values
  ball.contactnum(bp, mode)         — number of contacts
  ball.contactnum.all(bp, mode)     — includes inactive contacts
  ball.contactmap(bp, mode, map)    — contact information mapping

sections.heading: Wall FISH Functions
sections.content:
General:
  wall.find(id)        — get wall pointer by ID
  wall.list()          — iterate all walls
  wall.num()           — wall count
  wall.maxid()         — highest wall ID
  wall.id(wp)          — get wall ID
  wall.name(wp)        — get/set wall name
  wall.group(wp, slot) — get wall group name

Geometry:
  wall.pos(wp, dir)     — position component (1=x, 2=y, 3=z)
  wall.vel(wp, dir)     — velocity component
  wall.disp(wp, dir)    — displacement component
  wall.spin(wp, dir)    — angular velocity (3D)
  wall.closed(wp)       — is closed?
  wall.convex(wp)       — is convex?
  wall.cutoff(wp)       — cutoff angle
  wall.inside(wp, pos)  — is point inside wall?

Force:
  wall.force.contact(wp, dir) — contact force component on wall
  wall.force.app(wp, dir)     — applied force component

Servo (PFC5 specific):
  wall.servo.active(wp)        — get/set servo activation (0/1)
  wall.servo.force(wp)         — get/set target force magnitude
  wall.servo.gain(wp)          — get current gain value (read-only)
  wall.servo.gainfactor(wp)    — get/set gain relaxation factor (default 0.8)
  wall.servo.gainupdate(wp)    — get/set gain update interval (default 1)
  wall.servo.vmax(wp)          — get/set maximum velocity
  wall.vertexlist(wp)          — get list of wall vertices
  wall.facetlist(wp)           — get list of wall facets

Facet-level:
  wall.facet.id(facet)                        — facet ID
  wall.facet.pos(facet, dir)                  — facet centroid
  wall.facet.normal(facet, dir)               — facet normal
  wall.facet.vertex(facet, index, dir)        — facet vertex position
  wall.facet.vertexlist(facet)                — list of vertices
  wall.facet.wall(facet)                      — parent wall pointer
  wall.facet.area(facet)                      — facet area
  wall.facet.active(facet)                    — active status

Vertex-level:
  wall.vertex.pos(vertex, dir)                — vertex position
  wall.vertex.id(vertex)                      — vertex ID
  wall.vertex.delete(vertex)                  — delete vertex

sections.heading: Contact FISH Functions
sections.content:
contact.find(id)                — get contact pointer by ID
  contact.list()                  — iterate all contacts
  contact.num()                   — contact count
  contact.maxid()                 — highest contact ID
  contact.id(cp)                  — get contact ID
  contact.force(cp, dir)          — contact force component
  contact.moment(cp, dir)         — contact moment (3D, for bonded models)
  contact.model(cp)               — contact model name string
  contact.prop(cp, name)          — get contact property value
  contact.type(cp)                — contact type: 'ball-ball', 'ball-facet', etc.
  contact.active(cp)              — active status (0/1)
  contact.broken(cp)              — bond broken status (0/1)
  contact.gap(cp)                 — gap distance
  contact.end1(cp)                — first entity pointer
  contact.end2(cp)                — second entity pointer
  contact.dfn(cp)                 — associated DFN fracture ID (if any)
  contact.extra(cp, slot)         — extra variable
  contact.group(cp, slot)         — group name
  contact.isgroup(cp, name, slot) — check group membership

sections.heading: Measurement FISH Functions
sections.content:
measure.find(id)          — get measurement pointer by ID
  measure.list()            — iterate all measures
  measure.num()             — total count
  measure.maxid()           — highest ID
  measure.id(mp)            — measurement ID

  measure.porosity(mp)       — porosity inside region
  measure.coordination(mp)   — average coordination number
  measure.radius(mp)         — region radius
  measure.pos(mp, dir)       — region center position
  measure.bcount(mp)         — number of balls in region

  meas.stress(mp, i, j)      — stress tensor component
                               (1,1)=xx, (2,2)=yy, (3,3)=zz
                               (1,2)=xy, (1,3)=xz, (2,3)=yz
  meas.stress(mp)            — mean stress (average of diagonal)

sections.heading: Math & Utility Functions
sections.content:
math.abs(x)              — absolute value
  math.max(a, b)           — maximum
  math.min(a, b)           — minimum
  math.pi                  — pi constant
  math.sin(x)              — sine
  math.cos(x)              — cosine
  math.sqrt(x)             — square root
  math.exp(x)              — exponential
  math.log(x)              — natural log
  math.log10(x)            — base-10 log
  math.random.uniform      — random number in [0, 1)
  math.random.gauss        — random number from Gaussian distribution

  global.dim               — model dimension (2 or 3)
  global.tick              — cycle counter
  global.mech_age          — mechanical age
  mech.age                 — mechanical age (alias)

  string.build(format, args) — formatted string
  string.length(s)           — string length
  to.string(x)               — convert to string
  to.number(s)               — convert to number

  io.out(msg)                — print message
  io.in(prompt)              — read input
  open(filename, mode, unit) — file operations
  write(unit, values...)     — write to file
  close(unit)                — close file

sections.heading: Domain FISH Functions
sections.content:
domain.min.x()            — domain x-minimum
  domain.max.x()            — domain x-maximum
  domain.min.y()            — domain y-minimum
  domain.max.y()            — domain y-maximum
  domain.min.z()            — domain z-minimum (3D)
  domain.max.z()            — domain z-maximum (3D)
  domain.min.x(n)           — set x-minimum
  domain.max.x(n)           — set x-maximum
  domain.condition(c)       — get/set boundary condition type

sections.heading: History Data Access
sections.content:
history.id(id)            — get history ID from 1-based index
  history.name(name)        — get history ID by name
  history.size(id)          — number of records
  history.time(id, rec)     — time at record index
  history.value(id, rec)    — value at record index
  history.max(id)           — maximum value
  history.min(id)           — minimum value
  history.mean(id)          — average value
  history.write(id, file)   — write history to file

sections.heading: The while_stepping Pattern
sections.content:
PFC5 supports while_stepping — a special FISH function that executes at the end of every timestep/cycle:

  define myservo
    while_stepping
    ... code runs every cycle ...
  end

Key points:
  - while_stepping functions execute at the END of each cycle, after the mechanical update
  - Multiple while_stepping functions execute in definition order
  - To remove a while_stepping callback: define the function WITHOUT while_stepping
  - while_stepping is equivalent to: set fish callback -1.0 my_func

For PFC5 command blocks inside while_stepping:
  define conf_servo
    while_stepping
    local wp = wall.find(3)
    local f = wall.force.contact(wp, 1)
    command
      wall attribute xvelocity [servo_vel] range id 3
    end_command
  end

sections.heading: PFC5 vs PFC7 FISH Naming Differences
sections.content:
PFC 5.0 FISH function naming patterns:
  ball.pos(bp, dir)   — vs PFC7: ball.pos(bp)[dir] or ball.pos.x(bp)
  ball.vel(bp, dir)   — vs PFC7: ball.vel(bp)[dir]
  wall.force.contact(wp, dir) — vs PFC7: similar (backward compatible)

PFC 5.0 uses 1-indexed component access (dir=1,2,3), while PFC7+ supports both component functions (.x(), .y(), .z()) and bracket indexing ([0], [1], [2]).

PFC5 FISH is case-insensitive (unlike PFC4 which was case-sensitive).

PFC5 supports local variables and function arguments (unlike PFC4 which had only globals).

PFC5 uses loop foreach (not the old ball_head / bp_next traversal from PFC4).

sections.heading: PFC5 FISH Statements (from pfc5help)
sections.content:
Complete list of all valid FISH statements in PFC 5.0, from pfc5help (fish_statements.html):

1. argument              — Create a local variable (from function arg)
2. caseof - endcase      — Conditional switch (caseof / case / endcase)
3. command - endcommand  — Embed PFC commands inside FISH
4. construct             — Construct a FISH structure (struct)
5. continue              — Skip to next loop iteration
6. define - end          — Define a FISH function
7. exit                  — Exit current function immediately
8. exit loop             — Exit from a loop immediately
9. exit section          — Exit from a section
10. global               — Create/access a global variable
11. if - else if - else - endif — Conditional execution (block form ONLY)
12. local                — Create a local variable
13. loop - endloop       — Loop: while/foreach/for(count)/general
14. section - endsection — Define a named section of code
15. while_stepping       — Mark function for automatic cyclic execution

IMPORTANT: 'if' is ALWAYS block form per pfc5help:
  if expr1 test expr2 then    ; 'then' at line end, body on next lines
      statements...
  else if expr1 test expr2 then
      statements...
  else
      statements...
  endif

The word 'then' may be omitted. NEVER put code after 'then' on same line.
Tests: =  #  >  <  >=  <=  (# means "not equal")
